title: Cloud Architecture & CI/CD Mastery
description: Targeted learning module to close identified skill gaps in Cloud Architecture and CI/CD pipelines for Alex Chen.

sessions.title: Cloud Architecture Fundamentals
sessions.objective: Understand core cloud patterns: CAP theorem, distributed systems, scalability strategies
keyPoints: CAP theorem and trade-offs in distributed systems
Cloud-native design patterns: CQRS, Event Sourcing, Saga
sessions.practiceTask: Design a distributed system architecture for a ride-sharing app — choose your CAP trade-offs and justify them

sessions.title: Kubernetes Deep Dive
sessions.objective: Master Kubernetes workload management: Deployments, StatefulSets, Services, and Ingress
keyPoints: Deployments vs StatefulSets — when to use each
Pod scheduling, resource limits, and QoS classes
sessions.practiceTask: Deploy a 3-tier application (frontend, API, database) on a local Kubernetes cluster using minikube

sessions.title: CI/CD Pipeline Engineering
sessions.objective: Build production-grade CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions and Docker
sessions.practiceTask: Build a complete GitHub Actions pipeline: lint → test → build Docker image → push to registry → deploy with canary strategy

sessions.title: Database Optimization & Read Replicas
sessions.objective: Master PostgreSQL performance: indexing, read replicas, query optimization
keyPoints: Index types: B-tree, Hash, GiST, partial indexes
Read replica setup and routing strategies
sessions.practiceTask: Optimize a slow-running PostgreSQL query from 3s to under 100ms using proper indexing and query restructuring

learningOutcomes: Design distributed systems with confident CAP trade-off decisions
Deploy and manage containerized applications on Kubernetes
Build automated CI/CD pipelines with canary deployments
Optimize database performance using indexing and read replicas

title: Data-Driven Product Management
description: Targeted module to strengthen data analysis, A/B testing, and statistical thinking for Priya Sharma.

sessions.title: Statistical Thinking for Product Managers
sessions.objective: Understand statistical concepts that drive good product decisions
keyPoints: Statistical significance: what p < 0.05 actually means
Sample size calculation — avoiding underpowered tests
Type I and Type II errors in A/B testing
sessions.practiceTask: Audit 3 A/B tests from your backlog — calculate minimum sample sizes and check if they were statistically valid

sessions.title: A/B Testing in Practice
sessions.objective: Run rigorous A/B experiments from hypothesis to ship decision
keyPoints: Novelty effects and how to account for them
When to stop a test early — and when not to
sessions.practiceTask: Design a complete A/B test plan for a checkout flow change: hypothesis, metrics, sample size, duration, success criteria

sessions.title: SQL for Product Analytics
sessions.objective: Write SQL queries to independently answer product questions without waiting for data team
keyPoints: Revenue metrics: MRR, ARR, LTV, churn rate
sessions.practiceTask: Write 5 SQL queries that answer: retention by cohort, activation funnel, feature adoption rate, NPS correlation, and revenue churn

learningOutcomes: Design and interpret A/B tests with statistical rigor
Write complex SQL queries to independently extract product insights
Apply statistical thinking to product decisions with confidence
